prs.body:
> **Adding or modifying a card/dashboard?** Read the [Card Development Guide](.github/CARD_DEVELOPMENT_GUIDE.md) first — it covers required patterns, common pitfalls, and the full file checklist.

> **New CNCF project card?** New cards go in [kubestellar/console-marketplace](https://github.com/kubestellar/console-marketplace), not this repo. PRs adding new cards here will be redirected.

> **Use a coding agent.** This repo is primarily developed with [Claude Code](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) (Opus 4.5/4.6). It knows all codebase patterns (isDemoData, useCardLoadingState, locale strings, DCO). Manual PRs that miss required patterns will be sent back.

### 📌 Fixes

Fixes #12701 

---

### 📝 Summary of Changes

- Multi-cluster insight cards now pass full loading signals into card loading state: refreshing, failure flag, failure streak.
- This fixes refresh-state handling consistency across insight cards, not only initial loading/demo state.
- Card loading standard test now includes multi-cluster insights hook in detection logic, so missing loading-state wiring gets caught in future.
- Net effect: refresh UX + failure UX more reliable, regression guard

---

### Changes Made

<!-- Provide a detailed list of changes made in this PR. -->

- [ ] Updated ...
- [ ] Refactored ...
- [x] Fixed ...
- [ ] Added tests for ...

---

### Checklist

Please ensure the following before submitting your PR:

- [x] I used a coding agent (Claude Code, Copilot, Gemini, or Codex) to generate/review this code
- [x] I have reviewed the project's contribution guidelines
- [ ] New cards target [console-marketplace](https://github.com/kubestellar/console-marketplace), not this repo
- [x] isDemoData is wired correctly (cards show Demo badge when using demo data)
- [x] I have written unit tests for the changes (if applicable)
- [x] I have tested the changes locally and ensured they work as expected
- [x] All commits are signed with DCO (`git commit -s`)

---

### Screenshots or Logs (if applicable)

<!-- Add any relevant screenshots or logs to help visualize/test the changes. -->

---

### 👀 Reviewer Notes

_Add any special notes for the reviewer here_

prs.title: [BUG] Align VECM API with sktime fit/predict data flow
prs.body:
…fc to predict()

- Remove exog_coint and exog_coint_fc from __init__() parameters
- Initialize private variables _exog_coint and _exog_coint_fc to None
- Add fit() override to accept exog_coint parameter
- Add predict() override to accept exog_coint_fc parameter
- Add predict_interval() override to accept exog_coint_fc parameter
- Update _fit(), _predict(), and _predict_interval() to use private variables
- Update docstring to clarify that exog_coint is passed to fit() and exog_coint_fc to predict()

This change aligns VECM with sktime's API paradigm where data should only be provided at fit/predict time, not during initialization. Fixes #<issue_number>

<!--
Welcome to sktime, and thanks for contributing!
Please have a look at our contribution guide:
https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/get_involved/contributing.html
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.

Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests.
If no issue exists, you can open one here: https://github.com/sktime/sktime/issues
-->
Fixes #10189 


#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you have implemented.
-->
VECM API corrected to match sktime contract: model config in init, data in fit/predict.
Changes in [vecm.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html):

Remove exog_coint, exog_coint_fc from init params
Add private state holders _exog_coint, _exog_coint_fc
Override fit to accept exog_coint, store for internal _fit
Override predict to accept exog_coint_fc, store for internal _predict
Override predict_interval to accept exog_coint_fc, store for internal _predict_interval
Update _fit/_predict/_predict_interval to use private holders
Update class docs: cointegration exog goes through fit/predict, not init
Behavior now:

Old: VECM(exog_coint=..., exog_coint_fc=...)
New: VECM().fit(..., exog_coint=...).predict(..., exog_coint_fc=...)

#### Does your contribution introduce a new dependency? If yes, which one?
No new dependency.
<!--
Only relevant if you changed pyproject.toml.
We try to minimize dependencies in the core dependency set. There
are also further specific instructions to follow for soft dependencies.
See here for handling dependencies in sktime: https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/developer_guide/dependencies.html
-->

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?
API surface change correctness for fit/predict/predict_interval signatures
Backward-compat implications from removing init params
Internal state flow: no stale exog_coint/exog_coint_fc across calls
Compatibility with BaseForecaster and _StatsModelsAdapter expectations
Doc accuracy for new calling pattern
<!-- This section is particularly useful if you have a pull request that is still in development. You can guide the reviews to focus on the parts that are ready for their comments. We suggest using bullets (indicated by * or -) and filled checkboxes [x] here -->

#### Did you add any tests for the change?
No new automated test committed in this PR.
Reason: local env missing skbase blocked runtime test execution.
Code-level verification done for signature and internal usage rewiring.
If maintainers want, I can add/adjust targeted unit tests in [test_vecm.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) in follow-up commit.
<!-- This section is useful if you have added a test in addition to the existing ones. This will ensure that further changes to these files won't introduce the same kind of bug. It is considered good practice to add tests with newly added code to enforce the fact that the code actually works. This will reduce the chance of introducing logical bugs.
-->

#### Any other comments?
This is intentional API-alignment change. User code passing exog_coint or exog_coint_fc in init will need migration to fit/predict call sites.
<!--
We value all user contributions, no matter how small or complex they are. If you have any questions, feel free to post
in the dev-chat channel on the sktime discord https://discord.com/invite/54ACzaFsn7. If we are slow to review (>3 working days), likewise feel free to ping us on discord. Thank you for your understanding during the review process.
-->

#### PR checklist
<!--
Please go through the checklist below. Please feel free to remove points if they are not applicable.
-->

##### For all contributions
- [ ] I've added myself to the [list of contributors](https://github.com/sktime/sktime/blob/main/CONTRIBUTORS.md) with any new badges I've earned :-)
  How to: add yourself to the [all-contributors file](https://github.com/sktime/sktime/blob/main/.all-contributorsrc) in the `sktime` root directory (not the `CONTRIBUTORS.md`). Common badges: `code` - fixing a bug, or adding code logic. `doc` - writing or improving documentation or docstrings. `bug` - reporting or diagnosing a bug (get this plus `code` if you also fixed the bug in the PR).`maintenance` - CI, test framework, release.
  See here for [full badge reference](https://github.com/all-contributors/all-contributors/blob/master/docs/emoji-key.md)
- [ ] Optionally, for added estimators: I've added myself and possibly to the `maintainers` tag - do this if you want to become the owner or maintainer of an estimator you added.
  See here for further details on the [algorithm maintainer role](https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/get_involved/governance.html#algorithm-maintainers).
- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG]. [BUG] - bugfix, [MNT] - CI, test framework, [ENH] - adding or improving code, [DOC] - writing or improving documentation or docstrings.

##### For new estimators
- [ ] I've added the estimator to the API reference - in `docs/source/api_reference/taskname.rst`, follow the pattern.
- [ ] I've added one or more illustrative usage examples to the docstring, in a pydocstyle compliant `Examples` section.
- [ ] If the estimator relies on a soft dependency, I've set the `python_dependencies` tag and ensured
  dependency isolation, see the [estimator dependencies guide](https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/developer_guide/dependencies.html#adding-a-soft-dependency).

<!--
Thanks for contributing!
-->

prs.title: feat: Add waiting room DO and courses flows across learner and host journeys
prs.body:
This PR establishes the core discovery-to-course flow for Alpha One Labs by connecting the Waiting Room, Courses, and Teaching journeys end to end. It introduces dedicated pages for room detail, course listing, and teach-course creation, and adds backend support so creators can fulfill demand from waiting rooms into actual courses.

**What’s Implemented**
Waiting Room + Course Conversion Flow
Added waiting room detail experience and clickable room cards.
Added support for marking a waiting room as fulfilled with linked course metadata.
Exposed room-level fetch APIs for detail rendering and creator actions.

**Courses Experience**
Added dedicated Courses listing page and scripts.
Added Teach Course flow for instructors/hosts.
Integrated navigation updates so users can move smoothly across dashboard, waiting room, and courses.

**Host Management Controls**
Added host-side delete actions for:
Entire course/activity
Individual sessions within a course
Added corresponding backend endpoints with authorization checks and ownership validation.

**Impact / Review Focus**
Waiting room fulfill lifecycle and creator-only authorization.
Correctness of delete flows (activity/session) and data cleanup.

**Demo**
https://drive.google.com/file/d/1vQNKKGjwZYwT8LtRwKQEpbIl13ui2Djn/view?usp=sharing





<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Overview

This PR connects the waiting room, courses, and teaching journeys end-to-end by introducing new UI pages, client-side scripts, and backend endpoints that enable learners to request learning content and instructors to create courses from demand signals.

## Key Additions

### Frontend – Waiting Room Experience
- **Waiting Room Listing** (`waitingroom.html`, `waitingroom.js`): New landing page displaying open/fulfilled rooms with join/leave participation controls, room creation modal, polling for real-time updates, and toast notifications for user actions.
- **Waiting Room Detail** (`waitingroom-detail.html`, `waitingroom-detail.js`): New detail view showing room metadata (title, description, creator, participants, tags), participant list, join/leave toggles, and creator-only actions to teach a course or delete the room.

### Frontend – Courses & Teaching
- **Courses Listing** (`courses.html`, `courses.js`): New page displaying the authenticated user's courses with expandable session details, join CTAs for non-enrolled users, and navigation to course creation.
- **Teach Course** (`teach-course.html`, `teach-course.js`): New form-based UI for instructors to create courses directly from waiting room demand. Flow validates room ownership, pre-populates course metadata from the waiting room, collects course details and session times, creates the activity and sessions, then fulfills the waiting room to link course and demand together.

### Frontend – Course Detail
- **Activity/Course Detail** (`course.html`): New page displaying course metadata, session list, and host-specific delete controls for individual sessions or entire courses. Enrolled users see participation status; non-enrolled users see join CTAs; hosts see management actions.

### Backend – Durable Object & Deletion Endpoints
- **WaitingRoomDO** in `src/worker.py`: New Durable Object class implementing `/api/waitingroom/*` routes for CRUD operations (get/list/create/join/leave/delete) and fulfillment (`/api/waitingroom/fulfill`), storing room state with participant tracking and fulfillment metadata.
- **Deletion Endpoints** in `src/worker.py`: New `POST /api/activities/delete` and `POST /api/sessions/delete` endpoints enforcing host ownership, validating required IDs, and cascading deletes of dependent data with error handling.

## Impact

**User Experience**: Learners can now create learning requests (waiting rooms), see other open requests, join them to express interest, and view when instructors fulfill demand by creating courses. Instructors can browse waiting room demand, create courses directly from demand signals with pre-filled metadata, and manage course/session lifecycle with delete controls.

**Functionality**: Introduces a complete demand-to-supply pipeline where waiting room fulfillment triggers course creation and links the two entities, with polling-based real-time synchronization across list and detail views. Host-side deletion includes proper authorization checks and cascading cleanup of related sessions/enrollments.

**Authorization**: Creator-only actions (teach, delete rooms) and host-only actions (manage/delete courses and sessions) are validated server-side via token verification and ownership checks.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix: TreeExplainer series input for XGBoost
prs.body:
## Overview

Closes #4214  <!--Add issue number here, or delete as appropriate-->

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:
This PR fixes the XGBoost TreeExplainer failure when a single pandas row is passed as a Series. The explainer now normalizes Series input into a one-row DataFrame before DMatrix construction, preventing object-dtype errors in the XGBoost path. A regression test was added to lock this behavior.



## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

prs.title: [ENH] Defensive input validation
prs.body:
<!--
Welcome to pyaptamer, and thanks for contributing!
Please have a look at our contribution guide:
https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/get_involved/contributing.html
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.

Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests.
If no issue exists, you can open one here: https://github.com/gc-os-ai/pyaptamer/issues
-->
Fixes #284 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you have implemented.
-->
This change adds defensive input validation at the model boundaries so bad inputs fail early with clear errors instead of deep PyTorch/backend errors.

Main implementation:

New shared validator module in [_validation.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
AptaTrans validation hooked into [_model.py:346](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
InteractionMap validation hooked into [_interaction_map.py:30](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
AptaNetMLP validation hooked into [_aptanet_nn.py:103](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
What is now validated:

Empty tensors / zero-sized dimensions
Wrong tensor rank (2D vs 3D where expected)
NaN/Inf in floating inputs
Batch-size consistency between paired inputs
Feature-dimension consistency for interaction maps
Practical effect:

Short/invalid upstream outputs are rejected with explicit ValueError messages near entry points, improving debuggability for users of exposed APIs.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?

<!-- This section is particularly useful if you have a pull request that is still in development. You can guide the reviews to focus on the parts that are ready for their comments. We suggest using bullets (indicated by * or -) and filled checkboxes [x] here -->
Validation scope and strictness:
Does it reject only invalid inputs and not valid edge cases?
Error messaging quality:
Are messages actionable and specific enough for users?
Behavioral compatibility:
One intentional change is InteractionMap mismatch now raises ValueError (via explicit validation) rather than assertion-based failure.
Placement:
Checks happen at forward boundaries, before expensive layers.
Performance:
Validation overhead is lightweight and only basic shape/finite checks.

#### Did you add any tests for the change?

<!-- This section is useful if you have added a test in addition to the existing ones. This will ensure that further changes to these files won't introduce the same kind of bug. It is considered good practice to add tests with newly added code to enforce the fact that the code actually works. This will reduce the chance of introducing logical bugs.
-->
Yes. I added/updated tests in:

[test_aptatrans.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
[test_all_aptatrans_layers.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
[test_aptanet.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)

#### Any other comments?
<!--
We value all user contributions, no matter how small or complex they are. If you have any questions, feel free to post
in the dev-chat channel on the pyaptamer discord channel in gc-os server https://discord.gg/7uKdHfdcJG. If we are slow to review (>7 working days), likewise feel free to ping us on discord. Thank you for your understanding during the review process.
-->

#### PR checklist
<!--
Please go through the checklist below. Please feel free to remove points if they are not applicable.
-->

- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG]. [BUG] - bugfix, [MNT] - CI, test framework, [ENH] - adding or improving code, [DOC] - writing or improving documentation or docstrings.
- [x] Added/modified tests
- [x] Used pre-commit hooks when committing to ensure that code is compliant with hooks. Install hooks with `pre-commit install`.
  To run hooks independent of commit, execute `pre-commit run --all-files`

<!--
Thanks for contributing!
-->

prs.title: [BUG] Handle failed RCSB downloads
prs.body:
<!--
Welcome to pyaptamer, and thanks for contributing!
Please have a look at our contribution guide:
https://www.sktime.net/en/latest/get_involved/contributing.html
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
<!--
Example: Fixes #1234. See also #3456.

Please use keywords (e.g., Fixes) to create link to the issues or pull requests
you resolved, so that they will automatically be closed when your pull request
is merged. See https://github.com/blog/1506-closing-issues-via-pull-requests.
If no issue exists, you can open one here: https://github.com/gc-os-ai/pyaptamer/issues
-->
Fixes #314 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
<!--
A clear and concise description of what you have implemented.
-->
This fixes a crash in [_online_databank.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) when [retrieve_pdb_file](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) fails and returns None. Instead of passing None into [pdb_to_struct](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) and getting a confusing TypeError, [load_from_rcsb](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) now raises a clear [FileNotFoundError](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) that says the PDB download failed.
I also added a regression test in [test_online_loader.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) that mocks the download failure and verifies the new error is raised.

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?

<!-- This section is particularly useful if you have a pull request that is still in development. You can guide the reviews to focus on the parts that are ready for their comments. We suggest using bullets (indicated by * or -) and filled checkboxes [x] here -->
Reviewers should mainly concentrate on two things: the new guard is in the right place and does not change the success path, and the error type/message are appropriate for callers who already handle missing files. This is a minimal fix, so there should not be any broader behavioral impact.

#### Did you add any tests for the change?

<!-- This section is useful if you have added a test in addition to the existing ones. This will ensure that further changes to these files won't introduce the same kind of bug. It is considered good practice to add tests with newly added code to enforce the fact that the code actually works. This will reduce the chance of introducing logical bugs.
-->
Yes, 1 test in [test_online_loader.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
Regression test for failed RCSB downloads Mocks retrieve_pdb_file returning None Verifies load_from_rcsb raises FileNotFoundError with the new message

#### Any other comments?
<!--
We value all user contributions, no matter how small or complex they are. If you have any questions, feel free to post
in the dev-chat channel on the pyaptamer discord channel in gc-os server https://discord.gg/7uKdHfdcJG. If we are slow to review (>7 working days), likewise feel free to ping us on discord. Thank you for your understanding during the review process.
-->

#### PR checklist
<!--
Please go through the checklist below. Please feel free to remove points if they are not applicable.
-->

- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG]. [BUG] - bugfix, [MNT] - CI, test framework, [ENH] - adding or improving code, [DOC] - writing or improving documentation or docstrings.
- [x] Added/modified tests
- [x] Used pre-commit hooks when committing to ensure that code is compliant with hooks. Install hooks with `pre-commit install`.
  To run hooks independent of commit, execute `pre-commit run --all-files`

<!--
Thanks for contributing!
-->

prs.title: feat: sniff web app
Add: 3D Virtual robotics maze
prs.body:
The 3D Maze app is an interactive web application that allows users to explore and navigate a virtual maze rendered in three dimensions. Users can move through the maze, experience immersive graphics, and challenge themselves to find the exit. The app is designed for both entertainment and educational purposes, showcasing 3D rendering, user controls, and real-time interaction within a browser environment.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary

This PR introduces two new 3D interactive experiences to the Botlab robotics playground, expanding the project from a single 2D simulator to a comprehensive three-experience suite. Both new experiences support optional MediaPipe hand-gesture control with graceful keyboard fallback.

### New Experiences Added

**3D Assembly Simulator** (`3D/assemble.html` + `3D/assemble-app.js`)
- Interactive 2D robot assembly workshop where users configure robots by selecting modular parts
- Part selection via toolbox UI (keyboard 1–5 or hand pinch gesture)
- Dual control modes: gesture-driven (hand landmarks with stability filtering) or manual keyboard (W/A/S/D movement)
- Real-time robot preview showing equipped modules and held objects
- Visual feedback: lidar scan results, cursor tracking, status panel

**3D Maze Challenge** (`3D/index.html` + `3D/app.js`)
- Immersive first-person 3D maze rendered via raycast canvas projection
- Procedurally generated, seeded maze layouts with collision-aware navigation
- Interactive gameplay: collect pickup objects, avoid/interact with wandering NPCs, locate and reach the exit
- Object manipulation: pick up and drop items using gestures (fist/land) or Space key, with obstacle-aware placement
- Completion detection and automatic world reset
- Dual control modes: gesture recognition or manual keyboard controls
- Rich visual feedback: HUD overlay, crosshair, depth-sorted sprite rendering, optional lidar contacts panel

### Technical Integration

**Gesture Recognition Architecture**
- Both experiences dynamically load MediaPipe from multiple CDN sources (jsdelivr, unpkg) with fallback chains
- Hand landmarks classified into movement intents (forward/back/left/right, hover/land) and object interactions (fist gesture)
- Stability filtering applied to promote reliable gesture detection across frames
- Graceful fallback to keyboard-only mode if camera access is denied, MediaPipe loading fails, or secure context is unavailable

**UI & Navigation**
- Landing page (`index.html`) updated with new tech-btn links to 3D experiences (with page transition overlay)
- Hub page (`home.html`) expanded with 3D experience navigation links
- New shared tutorial framework (`3D/tutorial.css`) with accessible, dismissible modal overlays
- Experience-specific styling (`3D/style.css`, `3D/assemble-style.css`) with responsive canvas sizing and navigation UI

**Documentation & Discoverability**
- README restructured around "Core Experiences" rather than feature lists
- Updated control guides for each experience type (2D simulator, 3D assembly, 3D maze)
- Refreshed technical stack highlights and getting started instructions
- Simplified project roadmap and browser support guidance

### Implementation Scale

Approximately 4,100+ new lines of code, primarily simulation logic and rendering:
- Canvas-based 2D and raycast 3D graphics
- Gesture stability and input processing
- Physics/collision detection and object manipulation
- State management for multi-experience navigation and configuration persistence

### User Experience Impact

- Expanded educational capability: users can now assemble robots and test them in a navigational challenge
- Enhanced accessibility: fully functional keyboard controls ensure gesture features are optional
- Low friction: no build system or installation required—all experiences open directly in modern browsers
- Graceful degradation: MediaPipe integration designed with multiple fallback paths to ensure usability regardless of camera or vision library availability

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Add Sphinx-based documentation system and supporting files.
prs.body:
## Describe your changes

Added a complete Sphinx-based documentation system under docs/ with .rst files, Makefile, conf.py, and supporting scripts.
Included [CONTRIBUTING.md](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) and [DOCUMENTATION_SETUP.md](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) for contributor and documentation setup guidance.
Added scripts for documentation and example runs.

## Issue Link

closes #61 

## Type of change

- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [x] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [x] 📖 Documentation (Addition or improvements to documentation)

## Checklist before requesting a review

- [x] My branch is up-to-date with the target branch - if not update your fork with the changes from the target branch (use `pull` with `--rebase` option if possible).
- [x] I have performed a self-review of my code
- [x] For any new/modified functions/classes I have added docstrings that clearly describe its purpose, expected inputs and returned values
- [x] I have placed in-line comments to clarify the intent of any hard-to-understand passages of my code
- [x] I have updated the [README](README.MD) to cover introduced code changes
- [x] I have added tests that prove my fix is effective or that my feature works
- [x] I have given the PR a name that clearly describes the change, written in imperative form ([context](https://www.gitkraken.com/learn/git/best-practices/git-commit-message#using-imperative-verb-form)).
- [x] I have requested a reviewer and an assignee (assignee is responsible for merging). This applies only if you have write access to the repo, otherwise feel free to tag a maintainer to add a reviewer and assignee.

## Checklist for reviewers

Each PR comes with its own improvements and flaws. The reviewer should check the following:
- [ ] the code is readable
- [ ] the code is well tested
- [ ] the code is documented (including return types and parameters)
- [ ] the code is easy to maintain

## Author checklist after completed review

- [x] I have added a line to the CHANGELOG describing this change, in a section
  reflecting type of change (add section where missing):
  - *added*: when you have added new functionality
  - *changed*: when default behaviour of the code has been changed
  - *fixes*: when your contribution fixes a bug
  - *maintenance*: when your contribution is relates to repo maintenance, e.g. CI/CD or documentation

## Checklist for assignee

- [x] PR is up to date with the base branch
- [x] the tests pass
- [x] (if the PR is not just maintenance/bugfix) the PR is assigned to the next milestone. If it is not, propose it for a future milestone.
- [x] author has added an entry to the changelog (and designated the change as *added*, *changed*, *fixed* or *maintenance*)
- Once the PR is ready to be merged, squash commits and merge the PR.

prs.title: Add: 3D Virtual Robotics Playground
prs.body:
Introduces an interactive 3D robot experience with gesture and manual controls, modular robot parts, and live sensor-style views for a more immersive robotics demo.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/921f76e7-15d5-4525-88bb-70ccab52329c



<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary

This pull request introduces a new **3D Virtual Robotics Playground** feature, providing an interactive browser-based robot simulation experience with gesture and manual control capabilities.

## Key Changes

**New 3D Application Module** (`3D/` directory):
- **app.js** (1,699 lines): Core simulation engine featuring:
  - Gesture-based control via MediaPipe Hand Landmarker for real-time hand tracking
  - Keyboard-driven manual control mode (for fallback or direct file access)
  - Modular robot assembly system allowing users to equip different parts (chassis, sensors, etc.) via pinch/click interactions
  - 2D rendering pipeline with multiple canvas overlays: main simulation view, camera preview, LiDAR/HUD status panel, toolbox UI, and robot camera viewport
  - Static scene generation with random obstacles and wandering NPCs for realistic interaction
  - LiDAR scanning simulation synthesizing nearby contacts for sensor visualization
  - Multi-frame gesture classification for stable control state recognition
  - Deterministic scene setup with fixed camera/world parameters

- **index.html** (28 lines): Application entrypoint with no-cache configuration, full-viewport canvas (1920×1080), camera video input element, and dynamic asset injection with cache-busting parameters
- **style.css** (26 lines): Responsive full-viewport layout styling with CSS grid centering and aspect ratio constraints

**Navigation Updates**:
- **home.html**: Added "Switch to 3D" button link in the Toolbox sidebar
- **index.html**: Added "Assemble a robot in 3D" navigation button on the homepage with page transition overlay effect

## Functional Impact

- Users can now interact with a 3D-style robot assembly experience using hand gestures or keyboard input
- The app auto-detects camera availability and gracefully falls back to manual keyboard mode
- Includes MediaPipe CDN loader with error handling and multiple fallback sources
- Gesture controls enable intuitive robot manipulation without physical devices
- Sensor visualization (LiDAR) provides real-time environmental awareness within the simulation

## User Experience Enhancement

The 3D Playground enriches the robotics demo experience by offering an immersive, interactive way to understand robot assembly and sensor-based decision making through either gesture recognition or traditional keyboard controls.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Remove contributions component and routes/pages
prs.body: ### Checklist - [x] Did you run the pre-commit? (If not, your PR will most likely not pass — please ensure it passes pre-commit) - [x] Did you test the change? (Ensure you didn’t just prompt the AI and blindly commit — test the code and confirm it works) - [x] Added screenshots to the PR description (if applicable) ## Purpose Remove the contributions component and its associated routes, pages, views, and templates from the codebase. ## Key Changes **Frontend Templates:** - Removed the "View Contributions" card from the homepage (index.html), which previously displayed alongside the "View Waiting Rooms" section - Deleted the entire contributor detail template (contributor_detail.html) that displayed contributor statistics with Charts.js visualizations, engagement metrics, and activity timelines - Deleted the entire contributors list template (contributors_list.html) that rendered the contributors directory with rankings, statistics, and GitHub PR data **Backend Routing & Views:** - Removed two URL routes from web/urls.py: - `contributors/` (contributors_list_view) - `contributors//` (contributor_detail_view) - Removed three functions from web/views.py: - `contributors_list_view()` - aggregated contributors by GitHub PR activity - `contributor_detail_view()` - rendered detailed GitHub contributor stats - `get_user_contribution_metrics()` - performed GitHub GraphQL queries for contributor data - Removed GitHub integration constants: `GITHUB_REPO` and `GITHUB_API_BASE` ## Impact - **Functionality**: Eliminates the contributors browsing and detail viewing features; GitHub API calls for contributor metrics no longer execute - **User Experience**: Removes the contributions section from the homepage, streamlining the interface to focus on waiting rooms, forum posts, blog posts, and success stories - **Total Lines Removed**: 1,041 lines across templates, routes, and view functions

prs.body:
Summary - remove the "View Contributions" card from the home page - remove contributors list/detail routes- remove contributors list/detail templates- remove corresponding views and unused GitHub contributor helpers Validation- poetry run pre-commit run --all-files

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary

This PR removes the contributions feature entirely from the website, including the "View Contributions" CTA and all associated contributor pages, while making targeted infrastructure improvements.

## Main Changes

**Feature Removal:**
- Removed the "View Contributions" card from the home page (web/templates/index.html)
- Deleted contributor detail template (web/templates/web/contributor_detail.html, 414 lines) which included contributor profile, metrics, and chart visualizations
- Deleted contributors list template (web/templates/web/contributors_list.html, 251 lines) which included leaderboard, ranking stats, and CTA
- Removed two URL routes for contributors pages (web/urls.py)
- Refactored contributor-related helper functions in web/views.py, removing old aggregation logic

**Infrastructure & Maintenance:**
- **Workflow improvements:** Enhanced bot login check in check-unresolved-conversations.yml to recognize multiple bot identities using set-based matching
- **Pre-commit robustness:** Improved Python interpreter discovery in .pre-commit-config.yaml with fallback chain (Poetry → virtual env → python3 → python)
- **Test enhancements:** 
  - Added GitHub webhook integration tests for cache invalidation verification (web/tests/test_views.py)
  - Enhanced tracker creation and embedding tests with proper mocking (tests/test_trackers.py)
  - Updated admin test redirect verification (web/tests/test_admin.py)
- **Python 3.14 compatibility:** Added Django template context copy patching in web/apps.py for test compatibility

## Impact

The changes complete the deprecation of the contributors feature, streamlining the application by removing unused pages and leaderboard functionality. Infrastructure improvements enhance CI stability, test coverage, and Python version compatibility. No user-facing functionality is retained for the contributions feature.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: ENH: add NHiTS v2 model interface support
prs.body:
#### Reference Issues/PRs
Closes #2059
Related: #1992

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.
This PR adds native v2 interface support for N-HiTS and wires it into the v2 package ecosystem.

- New _nhits_v2.py with v2-compatible forward pass, unified train/val/test step logic, and v2 defaults (stacks, pooling, downsampling, quantile output)
- New _nhits_pkg_v2.py package wrapper for estimator discovery
- Updated __init__.py to export v2 symbols alongside v1
- Added unit tests in test_nhits_v2.py

#### What should a reviewer concentrate their feedback on?

- API compatibility — conforms to v2 model conventions
- Forward/step correctness — tensor shapes for point and quantile losses
- Config defaults — n_blocks, n_layers, pooling_sizes, downsample_frequencies
- v1/v2 coexistence — clean imports and no regressions in __init__.py
- Test coverage — key behaviors and edge cases captured in test_nhits_v2.py

#### Did you add any tests for the change?

Yes.

Added:

1. [test_nhits_v2.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) with tests for:

- initialization
- forward output shape
- quantile output shape
- step training behavior

#### Any other comments?
This PR is designed to be backward compatible for existing v1 users. The existing v1 NHiTS path remains available, while v2 support is introduced alongside it. No intentional breaking changes were introduced in this migration slice.

#### PR checklist
<!--
Please go through the checklist below. Please feel free to remove points if they are not applicable.
-->

- [x] The PR title starts with either [ENH], [MNT], [DOC], or [BUG]. [BUG] - bugfix, [MNT] - CI, test framework, [ENH] - adding or improving code, [DOC] - writing or improving documentation or docstrings.
- [x] Added/modified tests
- [x] Used pre-commit hooks when committing to ensure that code is compliant with hooks. Install hooks with `pre-commit install`.
  To run hooks independent of commit, execute `pre-commit run --all-files`

<!--
Thanks for contributing!
-->

prs.title: Fix: role-state copying in base graph copy methods
prs.body:
# The following checklist is mandatory.

Your PR will be closed if you remove the checklist or do not answer the questions to a satisfactory level. Use of LLMs is **strictly forbidden** for any part of this checklist (including for improving language), and will result in a **ban** if we find any use of LLMs.

### Your checklist for this pull request

- [x] Have you followed all the steps from our [Contributing Guide](https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy/blob/dev/Contributing.md)?

- [x] Does the PR fully address the linked issue and is within its defined scope? If you are still working on the PR, mark it as draft.
- [x] Are all the GitHub Actions checks passing? If not, mark your PR as draft while you fix it.

Please answer the following questions:

- Did you use an LLM for any assistance with this PR? Please describe in **detail** (around a paragraph) how and what you used it for?  
(Yes. I used LLM assistance to speed up navigation of the codebase and to identify all copy() implementations affected by the recent with_role/without_role inplace behaviour change.)

- What steps have you taken to verify that the changes correctly address the issue? And what edge cases have you considered? Other than running tests, what else have you verified?  
(I reproduced the issue scenario by checking behaviour of copied graphs when role assignments are mutated after copy, and verified that the original graph should remain unchanged. Then I updated copy() implementations to deep-copy node attributes directly so role sets are not shared between original and copy.)

- Has the LLM added try-except blocks? They will need to be removed; any error handling must be explicit.  
(No. No try-except blocks were added in this PR.)

- Have you used LLM for generating tests? They need to be compressed into a smaller number of tests without reducing coverage.  
(Yes. I used LLM assistance to draft focused regression tests. I kept them minimal and targeted without expanding redundant coverage.)

### Issue number(s) that this pull request fixes
- Fixes https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy/issues/2875

### List of changes to the codebase in this pull request

- Updated [copy()](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html) to deep-copy node attributes and stop role re-application in:
    
    [_base.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [DAG.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [PDAG.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [AncestralBase.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)


- Added regression tests to verify copy-role independence in:

    [test_base.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [test_DAG.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [test_PDAG.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)
    [test_AncestralBase.py](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)

prs.title: Auto-refresh contributor stats and stabilize checks
prs.body: Title: Auto-refresh contributor stats and stabilize pre-commit/test checks Description: Summary This PR makes contributor PR counts refresh automatically when GitHub pull request webhooks arrive, instead of waiting for cache expiry. It also fixes a few failing tests and hardens the local pre-commit hooks so the branch passes checks reliably in the current environment. Changes Invalidate contributor leaderboard caches on pull_request webhook events and after successful deploy pushes Add a regression test for PR-webhook cache invalidation Fix broken tracker and admin tests Add a test-only Python 3.14 compatibility patch for Django template context copying Update local pre-commit Django hooks to use the active Python interpreter more reliably Keep contributor leaderboard template/view changes aligned with the new yearly leaderboard context ### Checklist - [x] Did you run the pre-commit? (If not, your PR will most likely not pass — please ensure it passes pre-commit) - [x] Did you test the change? (Ensure you didn’t just prompt the AI and blindly commit — test the code and confirm it works) - [x] Added screenshots to the PR description (if applicable) ## Purpose Automatically refresh contributor leaderboards on relevant GitHub events and after deploys, stabilize local pre-commit/test hooks, and fix failing tests while moving leaderboard logic to a year-aware implementation. ## Key changes - Webhook-driven cache invalidation - github_update view now handles pull_request events (opened/reopened/closed) and returns appropriate responses; contributor caches are invalidated on PR events and after successful deploy pushes. - Added _invalidate_contributor_caches() and a year-scoped cache key builder _gsoc_landing_page_cache_key(year). - Added a regression test (GitHubWebhookTests) that posts a signed PR webhook and asserts contributor caches are cleared. - Leaderboard refactor and per-year support - Introduced _build_leaderboard_for_year(year) and helpers: _parse_github_date, _is_within_date_range, _fetch_search_pulls, and _should_exclude_contributor. - contributors_list_view and gsoc_landing_page now assemble, cache, and serve per-year (current and previous year) leaderboards and aggregate stats. - Template web/templates/web/contributors_list.html updated with a year-toggle UI and separate sections for each year’s stats and contributor lists. - Tests and test fixes - web/tests/test_views.py: webhook cache-invalidation test added; test setup populates caches and asserts they are cleared. - tests/test_trackers.py: fixed embed_tracker test by mocking web.views.render and verifying render args; create_tracker exercised via POST and response asserted. - web/tests/test_admin.py: admin add test changed to assert a 302 redirect Location header (no follow), matching admin behavior. - Pre-commit / local hooks - .pre-commit-config.yaml: django-collectstatic and django-test hooks updated to robustly detect PYTHON_BIN (poetry env, VIRTUAL_ENV, python3, python) and fail early with clear error if no interpreter found — improves local pre-commit reliability. - Python 3.14 / Django test compatibility - web/apps.py: test-only patch in WebConfig.ready() that replaces BaseContext.__copy__ with a safe copy implementation when running tests on Python >= 3.14, guarded by settings.TESTING to avoid affecting runtime behavior. ## Impact - Functionality & UX: Contributor leaderboards and stats refresh immediately after PR events or deploys, eliminating stale displays caused by cache TTLs. - Developer experience: Local pre-commit hooks and tests run more reliably across environments; regression tests and compatibility patch address previously failing tests (including Python 3.14-specific template context issues). - Maintainability: Centralized, year-aware leaderboard builders and cache keys simplify future extensions (additional years, filters). ## Notes for reviewers - Review web/views.py for correctness of new helpers, date filtering, GitHub event handling, and cache keys/invalidation paths. - Verify contributors_list.html yearly UI matches the new per-year context and that caching keys align with tests. - Confirm the Python 3.14 BaseContext.__copy__ patch is applied only during tests (settings.TESTING) and is safe for test environments.

prs.title: chore: streamline leaderboard data source
prs.body:
Why this change?
The leaderboard on the GSoC page was showing incorrect/missing PR details because links were scoped to the education repo only.
Contributors who opened PRs in other Alpha One Labs repositories were not reflected correctly when users clicked merged/open/closed/total counts.

What was fixed?
Updated PR link generation to query across the entire Alpha One Labs org instead of only the education repo.
Added date-aware query construction so season links match the leaderboard timeframe.
Simplified leaderboard data source to a single generated file ([leaderboard.json](vscode-file://vscode-app/Applications/Visual%20Studio%20Code.app/Contents/Resources/app/out/vs/code/electron-browser/workbench/workbench.html)) and removed old 2025 static data references.
Result
Leaderboard links now show PRs from outside the edu repo as well, so counts and click-through behavior align with org-wide contribution data shown on the GitHub Pages site.

Note
After merge, GitHub Actions will regenerate the leaderboard JSON. There may be a short delay before the latest data appears on the live page.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Purpose
Ensure leaderboard PR links and counts include contributions from across the entire Alpha One Labs organization (not just the education repo) and simplify the leaderboard data source.

## Key Changes

- CI / Workflow
  - .github/workflows/generate-leaderboard.yml: made the job repository-scoped, added BRANCH variable (BRANCH="${GITHUB_REF_NAME:-main}") and updated git pull/push to use the dynamic branch when committing regenerated leaderboard.json.

- Frontend / Leaderboard UI
  - index.html:
    - Removed the 2025 tab/data and now references a single generated leaderboard.json (2026).
    - Added LEADERBOARD_SEARCH_ORG = 'alphaonelabs'.
    - Added toDateOnly() and buildPrSearchUrl(type, username, startDate, endDate) to construct date-bounded, org-scoped GitHub PR search URLs for merged/open/closed/total.
    - Updated renderContributors(contributors, startDate, endDate) and fetchLeaderboard to pass season start/end dates so PR links open org-wide searches constrained to the leaderboard timeframe.

- Data generation / PR search
  - scripts/generate_leaderboard.py:
    - Added format_query_date() and build_search_query() to build org-scoped GitHub Search API queries with optional end_date and chosen date field.
    - Changed fetch_search_pulls to accept a pre-built query string.
    - Added MAX_CLOSED_PAGES and MAX_OPEN_PAGES safety limits and refactored pagination to loop-based fetching using the new queries.
    - Improved error handling (explicit 422 message for query issues; preserved 403/rate-limit checks).
    - Made build_leaderboard end_date optional and preserved existing aggregation/sorting logic.

- Deleted
  - Removed static data/leaderboard-2025.json.

## Impact
- Leaderboard counts and click-through links now include PRs from across the alphaonelabs organization and are date-bounded to the leaderboard season, fixing previously missing/incorrect PR details when users click counts.
- Leaderboard data is centralized into a single generated data file (data/leaderboard.json); after merge, GitHub Actions will regenerate it and there may be a short delay before the updated leaderboard appears on the live site.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Fix inflated PR counts on leaderboard
prs.body:
This PR updates the leaderboard generation script to use the GitHub Search API, which correctly counts only actual PRs instead of including individual file changes. I've also removed the local fallback code in index.html as requested to keep the changes as minimal as possible.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Purpose
This PR fixes inflated PR counts on the leaderboard by migrating from a counting method that inadvertently included individual file changes to the GitHub Search API, which accurately counts only actual pull requests.

## Key Changes

### scripts/generate_leaderboard.py
- **GitHub Search API Integration**: Replaces per-repository PR fetching with `fetch_search_pulls()` function that queries the GitHub Search API for PRs by organization and state, providing accurate counts
- **Authentication**: Adds support for GitHub API token via `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable for authorized requests and higher rate limits
- **Organization Scoping**: Introduces `GITHUB_ORG` constant set to "alphaonelabs" to scope data collection
- **Enhanced Parsing**: Adds `parse_cli_date()` and `parse_args()` helpers to support CLI arguments for date range filtering
- **API Pagination**: Implements pagination with `PER_PAGE = 100` and request pacing with `DELAY_SECONDS = 0.35` to respect GitHub API rate limits
- **Data Extraction Updates**: Modified PR data extraction to read `merged_at` from `pr["pull_request"]["merged_at"]` (updated field path from GitHub Search API response)
- **Robustness**: Updated `is_within_date_range()` to handle `Optional[datetime]` values

### index.html
- **Cache Bypass**: Appends timestamp query parameter to data fetch URLs to prevent stale data caching
- **Minimal Changes**: Removes UI scaffolding comments and simplifies markup while maintaining fetch/error handling logic

## Impact
- **Corrected PR Counts**: Leaderboard now displays accurate PR contribution counts using the authoritative GitHub Search API
- **Cleaner Implementation**: Eliminates local fallback logic that could cause inconsistencies
- **Improved Reliability**: Request pacing and proper rate limit handling prevent API throttling issues
- **Flexible Date Filtering**: CLI argument support enables leaderboard generation for custom date ranges
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Made the UI better and implemented the student leaderboard.
prs.body:
The current leaderboard is wrong with the number of contributors and total contributions, also displays the wrong order of contributors.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary

This PR transitions the leaderboard from a static, server-generated approach to a dynamic, client-side fetching architecture while improving the UI and project configuration.

## Key Changes

**Removed Infrastructure:**
- GitHub Actions workflow (`generate-leaderboard.yml`) that automatically synced leaderboard data every 6 hours
- Python script (`scripts/generate_leaderboard.py`) that queried GitHub API and computed contributor rankings
- Static leaderboard JSON files (`data/leaderboard.json` and `data/leaderboard-2025.json`)

**Added Features:**
- New `contributions.html` page with a complete dark-mode dashboard featuring:
  - Interactive student leaderboard displaying contributor statistics (merged/closed/open PRs, computed rankings)
  - Real-time PR statistics fetched from GitHub API (per-year support with dynamic year switching)
  - Client-side data processing and ranking computation
  - Responsive UI with animated cards, hover effects, and accessibility features
  - Particle canvas background and visual effects
  - FAQ section, GSoC timeline, mission steps, and social links

**Project Configuration:**
- Added `.gitignore` with standard rules for Node.js, Python, IDE artifacts, and environment files
- Added `.nojekyll` to ensure GitHub Pages serves all assets correctly
- Updated `README.md` with corrected Live Page URL and improved GitHub Pages setup documentation

## Architectural Impact

The PR eliminates the scheduled backend job dependency in favor of on-demand client-side GitHub API queries, reducing infrastructure overhead while maintaining real-time leaderboard accuracy. This resolves the previous issues of stale data and incorrect contributor rankings by computing statistics dynamically when the page loads.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: feat: Introduce two-command local development setup (#962)
prs.body:
# PR Title:

feat: Introduce two-command local development setup (#962)

# PR Description:

Resolves #962

## Description

This PR addresses issue #962 by introducing a standardized, two-command local development workflow. It drastically simplifies the onboarding process for new contributors by automating environment configuration, dependency installation, and database preparation.

## Changes Made

- Created robust `scripts/setup.sh`, `scripts/dev.sh`, and `scripts/doctor.sh` to automate environment preparation, running, and diagnostics.
- Configured `npm run setup` (via `package.json`) to safely duplicate `.env.sample`, generate secure secrets (`SECRET_KEY` and `MESSAGE_ENCRYPTION_KEY`), and apply development environment variables.
- Automated database migrations and demo data seeding (`manage.py create_test_data`).
- Included cross-platform support (macOS Gatekeeper binary clearance, OS-specific `sed` commands).
- Removed redundant comments in `web/views.py`.
- Added Devcontainer and docker-compose configurations for containerized setups.
- Implemented `onboarding-smoke-test.yml` GitHub Actions workflow to verify the setup process.

## How to Test

1. Clone the repository.
2. Run `npm run setup`.
3. Verify that the environment is configured and the Django system check passes.
4. Run `npm run dev` to start the development server.

This ensures a reproducible sandbox environment across machines and reduces setup errors.


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
  * Added a Docker-based local development environment and VS Code dev container configuration.
  * Introduced an onboarding smoke-test workflow to validate dev server startup.
  * Added setup, doctor, and dev helper scripts plus project tooling configuration and ignore rules for local deps.

* **Refactor**
  * Removed obsolete comments in view logic for clearer code.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.title: Local Development Sandbox, setup made easier
prs.body:
Problem

Currently, running the project for the first time requires multiple manual steps, including environment configuration, dependency verification, database preparation, and coordinating services. This often results in repeated setup questions, inconsistent environments across operating systems, failed first contributions, and additional maintainer time spent on onboarding support.

What this PR adds

This PR introduces an automated setup workflow that performs dependency and version checks, safely generates the .env file, initializes and migrates the database, seeds demo data, and verifies that the application starts successfully.

It also adds development orchestration that starts all required services together, waits for dependencies to become ready, provides clear service URLs, and handles clean shutdowns.

A diagnostic doctor command is included to detect common issues such as incorrect versions, ports already in use, Docker not running, or missing environment variables.

To ensure consistent environments, a devcontainer configuration and a Docker-based development stack are provided. Additionally, a CI boot test is added to prevent onboarding regressions by verifying the project can start successfully.

Usage

After cloning the repository, a contributor only needs to run:

npm run setup
npm run dev

to obtain a fully working local development environment.

Outcome

After this change, the project no longer requires a manual multi-step setup and can be started with a single workflow. The development environment becomes consistent across machines due to the reproducible sandbox. Most onboarding questions are reduced because common issues are automatically diagnosed, and first contributions are less likely to fail since the startup process is verified in CI before changes are merged.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added automated local development environment setup with integrated dependency management.
  * Added environment diagnostics tool to verify system configuration and dependencies.
  * Added development server startup script with health checks and pre-flight validation.

* **Chores**
  * Added VS Code Dev Container configuration for containerized development.
  * Added GitHub Actions workflow for automated setup and server health verification.
  * Added Docker Compose configuration for local development with database and cache services.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Enhanced user feedback when joining course sessions with next session details.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

prs.body: UI Enhancement: Branding & Social Integration This PR updates the landing page's overall UI and styling to improve branding and social presence. Key Changes: Hero Revamp: Integrated the "Alpha One Labs" logo and name inside the hero text. Re-ordered the system badge above the heading for better layout hierarchy. Footer Socials: Added YouTube, Facebook, Instagram, Discord, and X (Twitter) social links into the footer to expand the organization's reach. Branding & Styling: Registered image.png as the main site tab favicon, and updated Font Awesome to v6.5.1 to correctly render proper styling and the new X (Twitter) logo. Before: After:

prs.body: ## Related issues Virtual Robotics and Reinforcement Learning Sandbox #762 Fixes #762 ### Checklist - [x] Did you run the pre-commit? (If not, your PR will most likely not pass — please ensure it passes pre-commit) - [x] Did you test the change? (Ensure you didn’t just prompt the AI and blindly commit — test the code and confirm it works) - [x] Added screenshots to the PR description (if applicable) ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Virtual Robotics Playground: interactive 2D robot simulator with part assembly, keyboard controls, block pickup/drop, camera view, and live status display. * Added navigation link to access the Robotics Playground under Resources. * **Style** * Footer text wrapping adjusted for improved layout. * **Chores** * Development config changed to create project-local virtual environments by default.

prs.title: Fixed intermittent React hydration crash on macOS when changing respo…
prs.body: **Description** **Problem** The website intermittently crashed with React production errors: Error #425 Error #418 Error #423 This occurred more frequently when: Switching responsive viewports in DevTools (e.g. iPhone XR, iPhone 14 Pro Max, iPhone SE) Using a non-UTC timezone (e.g. IST – UTC+5:30) Using non-US locale or date formatting Running on macOS These conditions caused differences between server-rendered HTML and client-rendered output, resulting in hydration failures. **Root Cause** Viewport-dependent logic ran during SSR, producing markup that differed from the client render. Locale and timezone-dependent formatting (dates, numbers) produced inconsistent output between server and client. React hydration failed when DevTools device emulation or user locale differed from the server environment. ✅ **Solution** This PR ensures deterministic server rendering by: Deferring viewport-specific logic to the client side only Making locale- and timezone-dependent rendering consistent between SSR and CSR Preventing hydration mismatches by aligning server and client output As a result, hydration now succeeds reliably across: All screen sizes Device emulation modes Timezones Locales Operating systems (including macOS) **Tested On** macOS (Chrome, Safari) Multiple responsive device presets Non-UTC timezones (IST) Non-US locale/date formats **Related issue(s)** Fixes #4858 ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Bug Fixes** * Enhanced rendering consistency for date and time information in calendar and announcement components. * Optimized date computation logic sequencing in event post displays to ensure proper data flow during rendering. ✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.

issues.title: [Bug] Data compliance cards don’t show refresh or failure states clearly

labels.name: help wanted
labels.description: Denotes an issue that needs help from a contributor. Must meet "help wanted" guidelines.

issues.body:
The data compliance cards currently show install/configuration state, but they do not appear to surface refresh or failure state cleanly.

Affected areas:

DataComplianceCards.tsx around line 118
ComplianceCards.tsx around line 1095
When backing data is refreshing, stale, or failed, the UI can still look like it is showing a normal current install state. This makes it hard for users to tell whether compliance data is fresh, still loading, or unavailable.

Expected behavior
Cards should clearly reflect:

refresh/loading state
failed fetch state
consecutive failure state, if available
stale or unavailable data instead of silently showing old install status
Actual behavior
Cards mainly show install state, without clear refresh/failure feedback.

Suggested fix
Wire existing refresh/failure metadata into card loading/error handling and add regression coverage so future compliance cards do not miss these states.

issues.title: BUG: Multi-cluster insight cards ignore refresh and failure state from useMultiClusterInsights
issues.body:
Several multi-cluster insight cards call useMultiClusterInsights(), but only pass isLoading and isDemoData into useCardLoadingState().

useMultiClusterInsights() also returns:

isRefreshing
isFailed
consecutiveFailures
These are ignored, so affected cards do not show refresh state or failure state correctly when cached data refreshes or underlying cluster/event/deployment fetches fail.

Affected Cards
ClusterDeltaDetector
ResourceImbalanceDetector
DeploymentRolloutTracker
ConfigDriftHeatmap
likely other insight cards using same pattern

issues.title: Introduce two-command local development setup and reproducible sandbox
issues.body:
Setting up the project locally currently requires multiple manual steps such as configuring environment variables, installing dependencies, preparing the database, and coordinating services. This often leads to inconsistent environments and onboarding friction for new contributors.

This issue proposes adding a standardized local development workflow that allows contributors to run the project using only:

npm run setup
npm run dev

The setup process should automatically prepare the environment, initialize required services, and verify the application starts successfully. The goal is to make the project reproducible across machines, reduce setup errors, and lower maintainer effort spent on onboarding support.
